stem: A cloud computing server cluster uses 3 parallel nodes for redundancy. The request served requires at least one node to function. node 1 has a reliability of 83%. node 2 has a reliability between 68% and 81% (uncertain due to limited operational data). node 3 has a reliability of 85%. What is the overall probability that the request served?

ground_truth.answer: P(request served) ranges from 0.9918 to 0.9952. Point estimate: 0.9936.

solution_steps: P(system works) = 1 - P(all 3 nodes fail simultaneously).
At lower bound: P(all fail) = 0.17 × 0.32 × 0.15 = 0.0082. P(works) = 0.9918.
At upper bound: P(all fail) = 0.17 × 0.19 × 0.15 = 0.0048. P(works) = 0.9952.
common_failure_modes: Multiplying individual reliabilities instead of computing 1 - P(all fail)
Using a single reliability for the uncertain component without propagating uncertainty
Treating the system as a series (all must work) rather than parallel (any one suffices)
